behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the vision model’s real classification judgment plus policy handoff (flag -> hold). A stubbed classifier can always return "explicit" and hide real misses on actual explicit images, so Integration is insufficient. System was considered, but behavior text is about correctness of classification/decision, not OS/subprocess/network semantics; real model output is the critical dependency. Workflow rejected because this is one agentic decision path, not multiple distinct agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Submit known explicit images through moderation with the real vision model enabled; assert each is flagged and routed to human-review hold state, never published.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: This behavior directly tests robustness of AI text classification against adversarial obfuscation. Mocked outputs would mask true model weaknesses, so real model calls are required. System was considered, but the specific failure is model judgment quality on obfuscated content, not infrastructure behavior. Workflow rejected: it is a single moderation classification decision, not a multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run a suite of obfuscated hate-speech variants against the real text moderation model and assert each post is flagged for review.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is orchestration/state handoff across modules (image result + text result -> publish path), including preventing silent drops. This can be caught with deterministic safe outcomes from both checkers and in-process wiring. Higher levels were considered and rejected: Agentic is disqualified by behavior text "passes both content checks" (it does not require validating model quality, only downstream routing after pass); System is disqualified because no real OS/network/subprocess behavior is required to expose this failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Wire moderation service, decision engine, and persistence/queue modules with deterministic "pass" responses from both checkers; assert the post is persisted/published and not placed in review/limbo states.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end latency SLO over real pipeline execution under load, which depends on real process/network behavior and timing characteristics. In-process mocks hide queueing, network, and runtime contention effects. Agentic was considered but rejected because the assertion is infrastructure performance (time-to-complete), not model output quality; behavior text specifies completion time, not classification correctness. Workflow rejected because this is a single E2E timing objective, not validating multi-agentic decision quality across stages.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end load/performance test against deployed moderation components with real service calls and measure submission-to-decision latency; fail if p95 (or required percentile) exceeds 5 seconds.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is service-to-queue fallback logic when vision dependency errors. A deterministic simulated unavailability (timeout/error response) in module wiring is enough to catch incorrect auto-approve behavior. System was considered but rejected: behavior text targets fallback decision, not OS-level network pathology; real infrastructure is not strictly required to reproduce the logic failure. Agentic rejected because no model output quality is under test, only outage handling.
behaviors.test_description: Simulate vision model unavailability in the moderation integration (e.g., client timeout/error), then assert image posts enter retry queue and are not auto-approved/published.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real model behavior on borderline inputs and potential stabilization logic under repeated calls; mocked outputs would force consistency and hide true variance-driven failures. Workflow was considered because there are repeated submissions, but rejected: behavior is repeated evaluation of one agentic decision type, not a multi-stage agent pipeline with distinct agentic handoffs. System rejected as primary level because infrastructure is not the core uncertainty compared with model decision variability.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image multiple times within the target window using the real vision model and assert decision consistency meets the defined threshold/policy (e.g., identical hold/publish result each run).
